name: Priya Nair
projectIdea: Building an ML model that predicts campus dining hall wait times using historical data and real-time foot traffic sensors, so students can plan their meals without wasting time in line.
hobby: Bouldering at the Nick Recreation Center

name: Jake Sullivan
projectIdea: A full-stack web app for subletting off-campus housing — think Airbnb but specifically for UW students needing short-term sublets during summer or study abroad semesters.
hobby: Playing guitar at open mic nights on State Street
freeTime: Tuesday and Thursday afternoons, Saturday mornings

name: Aisha Johnson
projectIdea: A B2B SaaS platform for local restaurants to manage their inventory and reduce food waste by connecting surplus food with local food banks and student organizations.
hobby: Training for half-marathons along the lakeside path

name: Marcus Chen
projectIdea: An IoT sensor network installed throughout Lakeshore residence halls to monitor air quality, noise levels, and occupancy — giving students real-time data about their dorm environment.
hobby: Homebrewing craft beer and experimenting with Wisconsin hops

name: Sofia Reyes
projectIdea: An interactive sports analytics dashboard for UW Badger hockey, tracking player performance metrics and visualizing game patterns to help the coaching staff and fans better understand team strategy.
hobby: Salsa dancing at the weekly Latin Night events on campus
freeTime: Monday and Wednesday evenings, Saturday afternoons

name: Tyler Brooks
projectIdea: A mobile app for spontaneous study group coordination — you post a subject you're struggling with and your location on campus, and it instantly matches you with nearby students in the same class.
hobby: Competitive gaming (Valorant) and watching esports tournaments
freeTime: Afternoons most days, late evenings on weekends

name: Neha Sharma
projectIdea: Fine-tuning a large language model specifically for academic paper summarization and citation recommendation — helping researchers quickly identify related work and synthesize literature reviews.
hobby: Hiking sections of the Ice Age National Scenic Trail on weekends
freeTime: Flexible schedule — prefer weekend mornings or weekday afternoons

name: Ethan Park
projectIdea: A supply chain optimization startup focused on helping small Midwestern manufacturers reduce logistics costs using operations research algorithms and real-time freight data.
hobby: Exploring every coffee shop on State Street and rating their espresso
freeTime: Tuesday and Thursday mornings, Sunday afternoons

name: Lily Walsh
projectIdea: A predictive model for UW-Madison parking availability — using historical permit data and class schedules to forecast which lots and ramps will have open spots throughout the day.
hobby: Landscape photography, especially capturing sunsets over Lake Mendota
freeTime: Weekday evenings, Saturday mornings before noon

name: Raj Patel
projectIdea: Designing and implementing a compiler for a small educational programming language aimed at introductory CS students — something simpler than Python but more explicit about memory and types.
hobby: Playing in the UW Chess Club and analyzing grandmaster games
freeTime: Flexible most afternoons, prefer weekday meetups
